g.text: Fluorescence localisation via transient hybridisation of short imager strands to docking strands on antibody complexes
Docking strand intact · ready for next event
AINT - Unlimited Multiplexing with a Single Dye
ferent targets; wash steps remove the imager (not the docking strand) between rounds.
same dye · same laser · zero spectral crosstalk
same dye · same laser · zero spectral crosstalk
same dye · same laser · zero spectral crosstalk
Same dye and laser every round — no spectral crosstalk between targets
Each target has a unique docking sequence — orthogonal, no cross-hybridisation
ashing removes only imager — docking strands remain intact on sample
→ In principle unlimited targets; constrained only by available orthogonal sequences
→ Resolution: 10–20 nm in cells; sub-10 nm on DNA
Fluorescence intensity trace at a single docking site over time
